core_model.structure_summary: A system of coupled stochastic differential equations for an ordered set of interacting scalars whose drift combines a pairwise inverse-difference repulsion (the negative gradient of a logarithmic interaction potential) with a confining term, driven by state-dependent (multiplicative) noise, with an associated Fokker-Planck equation for the density.
core_model.canonical_form: dx_\alpha = \Big[\sum_{\beta\neq\alpha} \frac{g(x_\alpha,x_\beta)}{x_\alpha - x_\beta} - \partial_\alpha V(\vec x)\Big]dt + h(x_\alpha)\,dB_\alpha; \qquad V(\vec x) = -\tfrac12\sum_{\alpha\neq\beta}\log|x_\alpha - x_\beta| + \text{(confinement)}; \qquad \partial_t P = \partial_x[(\partial_x + 2V')P]

variables.meaning: ordered collection of interacting scalars (eigenvalue-like) undergoing repulsion and confinement
evolution parameter of the stochastic dynamics
coupling/monitoring rate and dephasing strength controlling regime (Coulomb gas vs exponential separation)
logarithmic interaction potential whose gradient sets the deterministic drift

notes: Central object is the Dyson-Brownian-motion-type coupled SDE for the ordered scalars (with the log-repulsion / Coulomb-gas potential and multiplicative Brownian driving), together with its Fokker-Planck dual for the density. The stochastic Schrodinger equation is the upstream source; the reduced eigenvalue SDE is what the main results (stationary inverse-Marchenko-Pastur law, exact finite-time joint distribution) are about.
